purpose: PreToolUse hook on Edit|Write. Blocks the master from writing initialization.json unless a properly structured plan.json exists in the same dashboard directory. plan.json captures the master's deep-thinking output (shared context + per-task approach/files) and is read by every worker on dispatch. Validates plan.json: parseable JSON, required top-level fields (name, created), context.prompt non-empty (workers need original goal), tasks[] non-empty, every task has id+title+description+approach+non-empty files. Cross-check on Write: agents.length in initialization.json content must equal plan.tasks.length. Edits to existing populated initialization.json bypass (repair/add_task path).
gotchas: Edit on already-populated initialization.json (task != null) is allowed — repair tasks and !add_task don't re-write the plan
Schema enforced is the dashboards/{id}/plan.json schema (NEW), not the legacy tasks/{date}/parallel_plan_{name}.md
Cross-check agents.length == plan.tasks.length is enforced on Write only — Edit may rewire agents (repair appends an `r` agent)
Block messages reference agent/_commands/p_track_planning.md and agent/master/initialization_blueprint.md for the canonical authoring guides
context.prompt must be NON-EMPTY — workers rely on it to understand the original goal even after compaction
patterns: Pre-write gate enforcing planning artifact existence (plan.json BEFORE initialization.json)
Per-task field completeness check via jq array-of-missings construction
conventions: Plan schema mirrors the spec in agent/master/initialization_blueprint.md verbatim
